other_observers: I called the Cheryl & Bill Day and Jason Beason. None of them were able to make it that day. Cheryl & Bill looked the next day, but did not find the bird.; I was the only person who saw the bird.
specific_location: slightly up-river of the confluence of the Gunnison & Uncompagre Rivers near the swinging bridge over the Uncompagre
description: This bird was feeding on Russian Olive fruit in the company of one American Robin. It was slightly smaller than the robin, but similarly shaped. It had a bright orange supercilium, throat and chest with a gray chestband and auriculars. It had 2 orange wing-bars and ornge at the bas of the primaries and secondaries. The rest of the bird including top of head, back, rump and tail were gray with a slight brownish tint. It is distinct from American Robin by its bold orange pattern on face and wings, and the gray breastband.Behaviors: It was feeding on Russian Olive fruit and in the company of another member of the same family.Call: silentPlumage: adult
